channel.title: Tham Le's Notes
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Tham Le's Notes

item.title: WTF: How Do You Actually Build a Distributed System?
item.description: From single server to distributed architecture — RPC, service discovery, replication, consensus, and the patterns that hold real systems together.

item.title: WTF is a Distributed System?
item.description: Why one computer isn't enough — and what breaks when you use two. A beginner's guide to distributed systems.

item.title: WTF is VAAPI?
item.description: How Linux offloads video encoding and decoding to the GPU — the VA-API abstraction layer, driver stack, and FFmpeg integration.

item.title: WTF is the Windows Clipboard?
item.description: How Windows stores, transfers, and negotiates clipboard data — from CF_UNICODETEXT to CF_HTML to delayed rendering.

item.title: WTF is the Windows Notification Area?
item.description: How system tray icons actually work — Shell_NotifyIcon, hidden windows, and the message pump behind the little icons.

item.title: WTF is the X11 Clipboard?
item.description: Why paste breaks when the source app closes — and the wild request/response protocol behind X11 selections.

item.title: WTF is PKCE?
item.description: Why desktop and mobile apps can't keep secrets, and the elegant trick that makes OAuth safe without them.

item.title: WTF is GPU Rendering in a Container?
item.description: How to render graphics inside Docker — from Xvfb fake displays to VirtualGL GPU passthrough to software rendering fallbacks.

item.title: WTF is JNI?
item.description: The bridge between native code and the JVM — how Rust talks to Java on Android, and why it's painful.

item.title: WTF is Cross-Platform Rust?
item.description: The reality of shipping Rust to Linux, Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS — cfg attributes, platform deps, and the pain points.
